p.em: At most one leader per term via quorum intersection

div.p: Mathematically: $Q > N/2$ Any two subsets of size $Q$ must overlap by at least: $(2 \times Q) - N$ nodes. $(2 \times 3) - 5 = 1$ node.
This overlap ensures that an old leader cannot exist in the same term as a new leader.
Node 3 remains leader but cannot commit any entries because it cannot reach a majority quorum. System remains safe.
Step-down Logic: Node 3 discovers Term 4 > Term 3. It immediately reverts to Follower.
